differentials.name: INHALATION of toxic fumes + gases =  alveolocapillary damage → pulmonary edema + bronchitis + bronchiolitis ± complicated by atelectasis + pneumonia
differentials.raw: INHALATION of toxic fumes + gases =  alveolocapillary damage → pulmonary edema + bronchitis + bronchiolitis ± complicated by atelectasis + pneumonia

context: Mosaic Attenuation / Perfusion =  CT imaging pattern consisting of a heterogeneous patchwork of normal and air- attenuated segments √  hypoattenuated areas with small vessels in 94% ← diminished blood flow √  hyperattenuated areas with normal / dilated arteries and enhancement in 77% ← increased perfusion The best method to differentiate between causes of mosaic attenuation is to perform expiratory imaging → thus allowing identification of areas trapping air. Pathophysiology: (a)  air trapping Cause:   any obstructive lung disease √  attenuation differences are accentuated on expiratory HRCT ◊
